data.title: BP reports power restored after outage at 440,000-bpd refinery in Whiting, Indiana
data.description:
NEW YORK (Reuters) -BP said on Friday power had been restored at its 440,000 barrel-per-day oil refinery in Whiting, Indiana, after an external power outage prompted a temporary evacuation at the plant.

"We activated our emergency response team and evacuated personnel out of an abundance of caution," the company said in a statement.

A spokesperson declined to provide more details on the units impacted by the power outage.

Last week, a fire broke out after an operational incident at BP's Whiting refinery, taking multiple units offline.

BP on Friday began the restart process of its 110,000 bpd fluid catalytic cracking unit after the fire, industry monitor IIR Energy said.

(Reporting by Nicole Jao; Editing by Kirsten Donovan)

By Nicole Jao

data.title: Consumer Sentiment Drops in October Michigan Survey -- Update
data.description:
By Matt Grossman

U.S. consumer sentiment declined in October and fell compared with an initial estimate published earlier this month, with stubborn inflation weighing heavily on shoppers' mood, according to the University of Michigan's survey.

The survey index ended October at 53.6, down from an initial October reading of 55 published two weeks ago and a drop from 55.1 last month.

The decline leaves the sentiment index hovering above the lows it hit in April, when President Trump unveiled steep new tariffs--but only just. That month, the sentiment metric dropped to an index value of 52.2 before recovering during the summer.

While the government shutdown has grabbed headlines, it was likely not at the forefront of consumers' minds this month, survey director Joanne Hsu said. Few respondents brought up the shutdown in this month's interviews, she said.

Instead, inflation and high prices remained their dominant concern, she said.

One-year inflation expectations declined slightly in October, the survey found. But consumers' long-term inflation forecasts moved higher--a troubling sign for the Federal Reserve, which prioritizes the public's confidence that in the long run, it will keep inflation under control.

Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1100ET

Consumer Sentiment Drops in October Michigan Survey
data.description:
By Matt Grossman

U.S. consumer sentiment declined in October and fell compared with an initial estimate published earlier this month, suggesting the toll from renewed trade tensions and an extended government shutdown, according to the University of Michigan's survey.

The survey index ended October at 53.6, down from an initial October reading of 55 published two weeks ago and a drop from 55.1 last month.

Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-24-25 1044ET

data.title: Banco BPM Repurchases 2026 Bond, 43% Investor Participation Reported
data.description:
(Alliance News) - On Friday, Banco BPM Spa announced the final results of its cash tender offer for the entirety of its bond issue denominated "EUR500,000,000 6.00% Senior Non-Preferred Notes due 13 September 2026".

At the expiration of the offer, the total nominal amount of bonds validly tendered for repurchase amounted to EUR216.4 million, representing approximately 43% of the total nominal amount of outstanding bonds, which stands at EUR500 million.

The offer was conducted concurrently with the issuance of new securities and aligns with the company's proactive management of its liabilities.

Banco BPM shares are down 0.3% at EUR12.13 per share.

By Michele Cirulli, Alliance News Reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com
